text.title: event-sourcing-projections Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for event-sourcing-projections as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Writing business logic inside projection handlers: if order.total > 1000: send_email(...).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Projections are replayed from offset 0 for rebuilds. Side effects fire on every rebuild, sending duplicate emails or charging payments. Business logic belongs in command handlers; emit a domain event for "high-value order" and handle it separately.

failure-mode.description: Projection with no checkpoint tracking — processing every event from the beginning on every application start.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a checkpoint, every restart replays the entire event history. On a long-running system this takes minutes and produces duplicate writes for non-idempotent operations.

failure-mode.description: Non-idempotent projection writes: INSERT without ON CONFLICT handling.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: At-least-once delivery guarantees an event will eventually be delivered more than once (after a restart or retry). A plain INSERT fails or inserts duplicates; UPSERT keyed on (stream_id, position) absorbs duplicates safely.
